MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.
Off Calendar Sep-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Todd Emanuel, Esq. of Emanuel Law Group, 650.369.8900, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Anna T. Pham, Esq. of LeClairRyan LLP, 415.913.4948, appearing for the Defendant.

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

Matter called, the Court'S tentative ruling is given in open court. The Court's tentative ruling is followed by ARGUMENT from counsel.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: mtorreano@sftc.org. =(302/RBU)

**After the hearing, the Court issued the following ruling: The City's motion for summary judment/adjudication is DENIED. The order prepared by the Court is filed this date. (see order for the Court's complete ruling)**

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re Fifth Cause Of Action (Declaratory Relief-Duty To Defend) Against Crossdefendant Thyssenkrupp Elevator Corporation
Off Calendar Apr-02-2018 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION (Renotice)Re Fifth Cause Of Action (Declaratory Relief-Duty To Defend) Against Cross-Defendant Thyssenkrupp Elevator Corporation
Off Calendar Apr-02-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Anna T. Pham, Esq. of LeClair Ryan, T:415.913.4948, for cross-complainant/moving party;
Christopher Olsen, Esq. of Clinton & Clinton, T:562.216.5000, for cross-defendant/opposing party Thyssenkrupp Elevator Corp, by Court Call.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling, as follows:

CROSS COMPLAINANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION on its fifth cause of action for declaratory relief for a duty to defend against cross-defendant Thyssenkrupp Elevator Corporation is GRANTED.

Based on the undisputed facts, the City is entitled to a declaration that Thyssenkrupp is obligated to defend it against the claims alleged by plaintiff Yvonne Karim in this case. Per the duty-to-defend language in the parties' contract, Thyssenkrupp became obligated to defend the City against the claims alleged by Karim in this case once the City made a demand that it be defended. All of Thyssenkrupp's procedural arguments lack merit. For example, neither party has raised an issue that would call for the contract's interpretation and the duty-to-defend provision is clear on its face.

I note that Judge Kahn has recently decided much the same issues regarding the same form contract in the same way. (8/29/17 Order in Pourjavid-Granfors v. Thyssenkrupp Elevator America, et al., CGC-16-550560; 10/26/17 Order in Caldeon v. Thyssenkrupp Elevator Corporation, et al., CGC-16-550973.) The City's request to take judicial notice of those decisions is GRANTED.

CCSF's request for costs was denied.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.
